Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to come up with a list of open ended questions to ask the interviewee about their property onboarding process The goal is to clarify and improve understanding by uncovering missing details decision criteria roles timing sequencing and exception handling No SQL or implementation details just conceptual

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to go through the process description again and identify areas where more information is needed Let me break down the process step by step

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Initial Contact and Documentation Review The landlord contacts the regional office and a property coordinator reviews documents Missing docs are requested But how are these documents verified Are there specific tools or checks Also what is the timeline for this step

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Property Details Entry Coordinator records details into the database What specific details are mandatory How is data accuracy ensured

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Property Manager Assignment Factors include workload geography experience track record How exactly are these factors weighted Is there a formal process or criteria for assignment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Introductory Meeting Align expectations discuss special conditions How are these meetings structured Are there templates or checklists used

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Photographer and Inspector Scheduling How are these professionals selected Are they in house or third party What is the process if the inspector finds issues

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Addressing Inspection Issues Who decides on the repairs How are contractors chosen What if the landlord refuses to make repairs

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Marketing and Activation How are pricing models updated What is the process for creating marketing materials How long does activation take

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Tenant Screening What criteria are used to select tenants How are conflicts resolved if multiple tenants are interested What is the timeline for this step

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lease Finalization How are lease terms negotiated What is the process for collecting deposits and scheduling move ins

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Quality Checks and Audits What specific regulations are checked How are discrepancies handled How often are audits conducted

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Exception Handling The process mentions special insurance or custom lease clauses How are these exceptions documented and approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Communication and Tracking What tools are used for tracking communication How are responsibilities divided between regional offices and support teams

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now I need to form these into open ended questions Each question should target a specific part of the process and aim to get detailed explanations Avoid yes no questions Let me start drafting

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For the documentation review How are the completeness and validity of the landlords documents verified during the initial review process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Property manager assignment Can you describe the specific criteria or scoring system used to assign a property manager based on factors like workload and experience

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Handling missing documents What is the process if a landlord is unable to provide updated documentation in a timely manner

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Property details entry Are there any specific data validation rules or mandatory fields that must be completed before proceeding to the next step

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Introductory meeting How are the outcomes or action items from the introductory meeting between the property manager and landlord documented and tracked

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Inspector and photographer selection What factors influence the selection of third party professionals like photographers and inspectors for the property

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Addressing inspection issues How are priorities set when multiple issues are identified during the inspection and who is responsible for resolving them

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Marketing timeline What is the typical timeline from when the property passes inspection to when it becomes active in the rental portal

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Tenant screening criteria What specific criteria or weightings are used when evaluating tenant applications especially when multiple qualified applicants are interested

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lease negotiation How are custom lease terms or exceptions such as special clauses insisted upon by landlords incorporated into the standard lease agreement

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Quality checks Can you walk me through the steps taken during the compliance and financial audits to ensure the lease meets all regulatory and business requirements

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Exception handling How are unique requirements such as properties needing special insurance handled differently compared to standard onboarding processes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Communication tracking What systems or tools are used to track and manage the communication between all stakeholders during the onboarding process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Regional office role How do regional offices coordinate with the central team during the onboarding process especially when dealing with local regulations or market conditions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Decision making authority Who has the final authority to approve a property for activation if there are discrepancies in the quality checks or audits

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Process bottlenecks Are there any known bottlenecks or common delays in the onboarding process and how are they addressed

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Stakeholder escalation What is the escalation process if there are unresolved issues between the property manager landlord or other stakeholders during onboarding

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Documentation updates How are changes to the property like updated inspection reports or new amenities reflected in the system after the initial onboarding

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I need to make sure these questions are open ended and not leading They should encourage the interviewee to explain their process in detail Let me check if I covered all the areas roles decision criteria timing exceptions communication etc

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Maybe add a question about how roles and responsibilities are defined between the coordinator manager and other teams Also perhaps about how the process varies by region or property type

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But the user mentioned not to ask about implementation details so focus on conceptual aspects

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Another point when the property is activated how is that communicated to the marketing team or other departments But maybe that is covered in the communication tracking question Also how are tenant applications managed once the property is active But the user wants to focus on the onboarding process up to activation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I think the list is comprehensive

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now to present them in a logical order perhaps following the process flow But the user did not specify order just a list So as long as they are clear and cover all areas it is okay

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Yes each question addresses a different step or aspect
